page: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server configured with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz, the instance features 1 physical core with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX-512_VNNI instructions. Local storage is provided via a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance characteristics. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng registers at 1455.08, placing it in the average tier, while multi-core performance drops to the weak tier at 2890.77 due to the shared CPU architecture. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with uncached read and write speeds at 16.98 GB/sec and 14.27 GB/sec respectively, both falling into the poor tier. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the lower percentiles, with static web serving reaching 2.61 GB/sec and Redis SET operations managing 2.96 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for lightweight workloads where dedicated resources are not required. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, lightweight background tasks, and small-scale database testing. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides fast disk access, but the shared CPU model and lower memory bandwidth make it less suitable for sustained high-throughput production databases or intensive computational tasks.
description: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor at 2.0 GHz. This configuration utilizes a shared CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads, supported by 16 MB of L3 cache and AVX-512 instruction sets. It includes a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with uncached read speeds at 16.98 GB/sec. This resource profile represents a budget-friendly option for non-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic background processing tasks where dedicated CPU performance is not critical.
